{"query":"Julian Assange public appearances statements after release 2025","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1262,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.washingtonpost.com/video/world/assange-makes-first-comments-after-release/2024/10/01/3f4048e9-60a3-417d-9b14-3489ae0c1751_video.html","title":"Assange makes first comments after release","content":"In Strasbourg, France, on Oct 1., Wikileaks founder Julian Assange spoke publicly for the first time since his release from prison in June."},{"url":"https://www.dw.com/en/julian-assange-makes-first-public-address-since-release/a-70372957","title":"Julian Assange makes first public address since release","content":""I am not free today because the system worked," Assange told lawmakers from 46 countries.
Assange said that moving from confinement in the Ecuadorian Embassy in London to British prison to addressing the council was a "profound and surreal shift," adding that the last few years had been a "relentless struggle to stay alive, both physically and mentally."
After his release three months earlier, his wife, Stella Assange, said that the famous whistleblower needed time to recuperate physically from his stay in the UK's maximum security Belmarsh Prison, where he was kept in solitary confinement."},{"url":"https://abcnews.com/International/julian-assange-makes-1st-public-appearance/story?id=114388868","title":"Julian Assange makes 1st public appearance since his release from prison - ABC News","content":"Julian Assange makes 1st public appearance since his release from prison
Assange told European lawmakers he was forced to "plead guilty to journalism."
I am free today after years of incarceration because I pled guilty to journalism,” Assange said at the hearing, which was broadcast live."},{"url":"https://apnews.com/article/julian-assange-statement-council-europe-5373cfb854474deba0e5799b23cb8d2e","title":"WikiLeaks' Julian Assange says he pleaded 'guilty to journalism' in order to be freed | AP News","content":"Wikileaks founder Julian Assange, center, his wife Stella Assange, right, and editor-in-chief of WikiLeaks Kristinn Hrafnsson, raise their fists as they arrive at the Council of Europe, in Strasbourg, eastern France, Tuesday, Oct.
STRASBOURG, France (AP) — WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange said Tuesday that he was freed after years of incarceration because he “pled guilty to journalism.”
The Parliamentary Assembly includes lawmakers from 46 European countries.
Assange pleaded guilty to an Espionage Act charge of conspiring to unlawfully obtain and disseminate classified national defense information.
Assange returned to Australia a free man in late June.
His appearance on Tuesday comes after the Council of Europe’s Parliamentary Assembly published a report on Assange’s detention in a high-security U.K. prison for five years.
Surk reported from Nice, France, and Hui reported from London."},{"url":"https://www.politico.eu/article/julian-assange-make-first-public-appearance-wikileaks-freedom-espionage-law/","title":"Julian Assange to make first public appearance post-release in Strasbourg – POLITICO","content":"Julian Assange, founder of WikiLeaks, is set to address the human rights organization Council of Europe next week in a first public appearance since being freed from a British jail, WikiLeaks said Wednesday.
“Julian will be in Strasbourg next week on October 1st.
The WikiLeaks founder returned to Australia in June after striking a deal that secured his freedom, but forced him to plead guilty to violating United States espionage law, ending a drawn-out 14-year-old legal saga.
U.S. prosecutors said the massive leak endangered hundreds of lives and that Assange was aware of the dangers, prompting a U.S. criminal investigation to prosecute him under the Espionage Act.
Assange has always denied any wrongdoing.
He has been held in Belmarsh high-security prison since 2019, when he was removed from the Ecuadorian Embassy in London, where he took refuge for years."}]}
